title: Pan Seared Hakka Pork Xiaolongbao
description: Rich and crispy pan seared Hakka pork xiaolongbao, a quick fix Chinese dish featuring chilled butter, made diabetic friendly and cooked in a wok.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine ground pork, chilled butter, ginger, green onions, soy sauce, sesame oil, salt, and black pepper, and mix well until just combined.
Lay a wonton wrapper on a clean surface and place a tablespoon of the pork mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Dip your finger in a small amount of water and run it along the edges of the wrapper.
Fold the wrapper into a triangle by bringing the two opposite corners together to form a point, and press the edges together to seal the xiaolongbao.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Heat a large wok or skillet over medium high heat and add a small amount of oil to the pan.
When the oil is hot, add a few of the xiaolongbao to the pan, being careful not to overcrowd, and pan fry until the bottoms are golden brown.
Add a small amount of water to the pan and cover with a lid to steam the xiaolongbao for a few minutes.
Remove the lid and continue to cook until the water has evaporated and the bottoms are crispy.
Serve the pan seared Hakka pork xiaolongbao hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
